task_completion.description: Navigate to the Sales Orders page in Magento Admin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Magento Admin panel is visible, and the 'Orders' page under the 'Sales' menu is currently displayed.
The 'Columns' dropdown menu above the order grid is clicked and expanded, showing a list of column names with checkboxes.

task_completion.description: Configure the grid to show 'Payment Method' and 'Shipping Information'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Columns dropdown, the checkboxes for 'Payment Method' and 'Shipping Information' are checked, OR these columns are clearly visible in the order grid header.

task_completion.description: Configure the grid to hide 'Grand Total (Base)'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Columns dropdown, the checkbox for 'Grand Total (Base)' is unchecked, OR this column is visibly absent from the order grid header.

task_completion.description: Open the View/Bookmark menu to save the configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The View selector dropdown (usually labeled 'Default View' or similar, next to the Columns button) is expanded, showing options to save a new view.

task_completion.description: Save the configuration as a new view named 'Logistics View'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Logistics View' is typed into the 'Save View As' input field, OR 'Logistics View' is shown as the currently active view in the view selector button.

integrity.description: The agent used the Magento Admin GUI to configure the grid.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Magento web interface (clicking dropdowns, checking boxes) rather than terminal commands or database scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Magento application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser displays the standard Magento Admin interface with proper styling, not a text editor, mock, or static HTML page.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser DevTools to fake the view name or columns.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the browser DevTools (Inspector/Console) being open to manually edit the DOM text to 'Logistics View'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target view name must be exactly 'Logistics View'. The required visible columns correspond to the UI labels 'Payment Method' (internal: payment_method) and 'Shipping Information' (internal: shipping_information). The required hidden column corresponds to the UI label 'Grand Total (Base)' (internal: base_grand_total).
